messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "band-top-contaminated-comparator",
 "title": "The €360M ceiling was set with the same contaminated-category trick it claimed to remove — the real base may sit above the band and the plan is sized for the wrong market",
 "narrative": "Refinement cycle 1 lowered the band top from €495M to €360M by comparing the BIS ceiling's 21% growth against a '10–12% intelligent-PDU CAGR (IndexBox)'. But that 10–12% figure comes from IndexBox's €8.5–9.5B EU 'Power and Cable Management' report — a category several times broader than rack PDUs — so the correction that purged one contaminated number imported another. Meanwhile Germany alone is forecast at $509.6M in a PDU category by 2031, already exceeding the entire CE-7 base, and the global anchors span $1.7–3.94B, a 2.3x spread wider than the band. If the true CE base sits at €400M+, the failure mode is not embarrassment — it is a Year-1 plan whose certification scope, channel headcount, and manufacturing commitments were sized for €165M of SAM while incumbents scale into the €80–120M the model never showed the board.",
 "category": "fact",
 "targetNodes": [
  "tamBase"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "The band-top-lowering comparator ('10–12% intelligent-PDU CAGR, IndexBox') is sourced from the EU Power and Cable Management report — a €8.5–9.5B multi-category basket — not a rack-PDU report, replicating the exact definitional bundling the refinement flagged in the BIS ceiling",
   "refs": [
    "digest:tam-base#c4",
    "ledger:tamBase"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Independent evidence points above the band: Germany's broader-PDU forecast alone reaches $509.6M by 2031, and same-category 2025 global anchors span $1.7B to $3.94B — a spread that exceeds the ±20% band and makes the €360M cap an artifact of one contested comparison rather than a bound",
   "refs": [
    "digest:tam-base#c9",
    "digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c4",
    "digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c9"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "With tamBase capped at €360M, SAM and the Year-1 obtainable plan are calibrated low; under-sizing systematically under-provisions certification breadth (serviceable reach) and channel capacity in the launch window, which is unrecoverable in a market where gear is specified 12–24+ months ahead",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "Downward refinements read as rigor — 'we caught an inflated ceiling and cut it' is exactly what a diligent team wants on the record, so nobody audits the comparator that justified the cut. Upside error also feels safe ('conservative is fine'), hiding that under-sizing misallocates real resources: too-narrow cert scope and too-thin channel for the market that actually exists.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "The IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report headline (once purchased) landing at or below €360M for the 2025/2026 EU base, confirming the lowered ceiling despite the contaminated comparator.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.3,
  "rationale": "Multiple broader-category datapoints point above the band, but each carries the same definitional contamination in the upward direction; the honest read is that the ceiling is unproven rather than proven wrong",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "tamBase",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 420,
   "note": "Base above the current band top, consistent with the Germany-alone $509.6M signal and the upper global anchors"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Directly-scoped EU/DE rack-PDU headline figures",
   "where": "IndexBox EU Rack PDUs and Germany Rack PDUs reports ($4,000 each); Omdia rack-PDU vendor share table",
   "threshold": "EU headline >€400M or Germany >€180M",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Incumbent EMEA rack-power growth outrunning the model's 9% CAGR",
   "where": "Vertiv and Legrand quarterly earnings calls, EMEA data-center product-line commentary",
   "threshold": "Two consecutive quarters of >15% YoY EMEA rack-power growth",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission the IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report to fix the base directly — it resolves this hypothesis and the Europe-share hop simultaneously",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "tamBase",
   "note": "The ledger's own promotionPath; a $4,000 spend against a €60M+ band question"
  },
  {
   "action": "Build the Year-1 operating plan with staged capacity options (certification scope and contract manufacturing volumes expandable at 6-month gates) rather than fixed to the €165M SAM",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Makes the plan robust to the base being above the band without pre-committing capital"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "definition-scopedown",
 "riskId": "risk.definition-scopedown.band-top-contaminated-comparator"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

Omdia rack-PDU vendor share table","threshold":"EU headline >€400M or Germany >€180M","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Incumbent EMEA rack-power growth outrunning the model's 9% CAGR","where":"Vertiv and Legrand quarterly earnings calls, EMEA data-center product-line commentary","threshold":"Two consecutive quarters of >15% YoY EMEA rack-power growth","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission the IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report to fix the base directly — it resolves this hypothesis and the Europe-share hop simultaneously","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"The ledger's own promotionPath; a $4,000 spend against a €60M+ band question"},{"action":"Build the Year-1 operating plan with staged capacity options (certification scope and contract manufacturing volumes expandable at 6-month gates) rather than fixed to the €165M SAM","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Makes the plan robust to the base being above the band without pre-committing capital"}]}
